question: Which FusedEpi 95% PI coverage is canonical, why two values, and does the PI under-cover?
canonical_source: R10 per_model_eval.py (per_model_metrics.csv, SSOT) — leak-free split-conformal + adaptive

r10_canonical_0p735.construction: per_model_eval.py:870-885 — K=11 split-conformal half-width (k11_pi_widths_from_residuals) over LEAK-FREE in-sample residuals; MPH_ADAPTIVE_CONFORMAL widening; coverage_with_exact_ci Wilson
r10_canonical_0p735.why_lower: narrow, honest, leak-free residual-quantile band on the n=68 test slab

r9_optimal_1p0.construction: per_model_optimize.py:827 — single symmetric band at the 0.95 empirical quantile of TRAINING-POOL |residuals| (coarser, wider)
r9_optimal_1p0.why_higher: wider band over-covers on the test slab; diagnostic, NOT the paper number

real_slab_aci_0p9375.construction: per_model_optimize.py:1799-1834 — ACI adaptive conformal over n=16 real-forecast origins; the '1.0' in the prompt was the optimal-JSON test_metrics over-coverage, real-slab ACI is 0.9375 not 1.0

reconciliation.verdict: 0.735 is correct and is genuine UNDER-coverage on the leak-free test-slab band; reported as-is (no inflation).

backtest_interpretation: Tests the SPLIT-CONFORMAL PI CONSTRUCTION (the method behind R10's 0.735) across 257 leak-free origins using a deterministic reference forecaster — NOT FusedEpi's specific bands (refitting FusedEpi 257× = retraining, forbidden). Finding: at 95% the method is well-calibrated (0.942, nominal inside Wilson CI); at 80%/50% it UNDER-covers (heavy-tailed/right-skewed ILI residuals → symmetric split-conformal half-widths too narrow in the body of the distribution). This is consistent with R10 FusedEpi: 95%=0.735, 80%=0.588, 50%=0.206 all under-cover, and the under-coverage worsens toward the center — the same directional signature.

0.95.note: closest achievable nominal differs from 0.95 by up to 0.023

0.8.note: closest achievable nominal differs from 0.8 by up to 0.023

0.5.note: closest achievable nominal differs from 0.5 by up to 0.023

calset_diagnostic.coverage_estimate_note: 0.735 is estimated on only n=68 test points → Wilson CI (0.50, 0.735) is wide; point estimate is under-cover but imprecise.

provenance.y_series: reconstructed run_data(seed=42), READ_ONLY DB, in-sample n=337
provenance.backtest_forecaster: deterministic leak-free seasonal-naive+AR reference (NO FusedEpi refit)
provenance.leak_free: conformal band at origin t uses only residuals on [0,t)
